Do you have a reading as to how the AI and the social world from the perspective of investment are going to or ought to or not to relate going forward to make this ecosystem here more investable than it is?
Sure.
The story that I tell about that sort of thing is platforms are being – there is a cat and mouse game for hacking the attention economy.
When you have a centralized algorithm, the target is bigger, the value of hacking it is, you know, it's more valuable, right?
So a centralized platform is always going to be at a disadvantage in terms of being able to prevent this sort of stuff.
Federating elections makes elections safe or unhackable in some sense, because there's a huge surface area.
Federating algorithms has a similar effect to be able to prevent this sort of stuff.

Also as, you know, AI-generated slop will infect any platform, the ability for individual curators to be reactive to that in their local community, I think, is going to be a much more efficient system than just having some sort of de-slop button.
And in general, the sort of shift is going to be from the value of creators to the value of curators that are able to filter this stuff out actively for their communities.
And all of that is going to be, you know, that is the effect of AI, and I think it will make this ecosystem grow.
To that same kind of point, do you think it's, do you think investment will eventually just catch up, or the ecosystem has to, like, change to fit the mold that investment sees as warranted?
